description: Math accents / vector marks (prime, dot, overline, tilde, double-dot, vector arrow). The accent encoding is base + position marker (above ⠘ / below ⠰) + the mark symbol; vectors →/← use the arrow mark, overline uses the dash, and single/double form is chosen by the base letter count. Known accents emit no warning; an unknown accent (e.g. the ^ from \hat) triggers MATH_UNKNOWN_SYMBOL.

known_clean.description: Known accents — output must be clean, no MATH_* warnings allowed

cases.note: prime ′ = ⠨⠔ (46+35, self-contained upper-right marker); as an msup superscript it skips the superscript sign ⠌
dot directly above = above marker ⠘ + dot ⠂ (distinct from the dot product ⋅=⠄)
tilde directly above ~ = ⠘ + tilde ⠢ (\tilde produces U+007E)
bar directly below = below marker ⠰ + bar ⠒ (munder path)
vector arrow mark, single letter: \vec produces → U+2192, remapped in the accent position to ⠘ + arrow mark ⠒⠂ (≠ relation arrow ⠒⠕)
vector arrow mark, two letters ⠘ + ⠒⠆ (base 2 letters, all-capital run shares the doubled ⠠⠠)
leftward arrow ← uses the same arrow mark (no separate left variant)
bar, two letters ⠘ + ⠒⠒ (base 2 letters, doubled ⠠⠠); compare \overline{x} single letter ⠒

unknown_warn.description: Unknown accents — trigger MATH_UNKNOWN_SYMBOL (the position marker is still emitted)

cases.note: hat ^ (U+005E) has no exact docx correspondence, not yet recorded → above marker ⠘ + unknown cell (see math-symbols-plan S5)
